2. when may a baIlot measure coramittee file a terrnination

This is in response to your letter of December l-0, 1990,
requesting advice regTarding the use of surplus funds and a ballot
meisure commj-ttee's reporting obligations under the PoIitical
Reform Act (the ttAct").1 Because your request does not involve a
pending governmental decision, we will provide you with inforrnal
assistance. z

An unsuccessful candidate may only use surplus funds for
specified purposes, including payment of outstanding carnpaign
debts, pro rata repayment of contributions, donations to bona fi'de
nonprofit organizations, contributions to a political 
party or
comrnittee, and contributions to support or oppose any candidate
for federal- office, out-of-state office, or any ballot measure.
2. A ballot measure comrnittee rnay file a statement of
ternination at any tiroe, so long as (1) it has ceased to receive
contributions and make expenditures and does not anticipate
receiving contributions or making expenditures in the future; (2)
it has el-iminated all debts or declared that it has no intention
or ability to discharge aII of its debts; (3) it has no surplus
funds, and (4) it has fiLed all required campaign statements
disclosing aII reportable transactions, including the disposition
of surplus funds.
FACTS
You are the treasurer for a city council candidate and a
ballot initiative 
conmittee. You hrould like to know the options
for the candidate's use of leftover campaign funds, and l{hether he
can save the funds for his next campaign or give them to a non-
profit organization which is succeeding the initiative 
committee-
You also want to know lrhether the ending date for purposes of
filing a termination report on behalf of the baIIot., measure
comnittee would be the election day.
ANALYSIS
The permissible uses for funds remaining in a campaign
account after the election depend on whether the candidate was
successful in the election.
A successful candidate may use the funds for any expenditure
that is reasonably related to a legislative, 
governmental, . or
political, purpo=el ff the expendiiure confers a "substantj'al
personal uineiit, 
i.e., a direct.personal benefit with a value of
more than $1oo to the elected officer, the expenditure must be
directlv related to a legistative, governmental, or politj'cal
F.d= 
(Sections 8g5L,. ) Please review Sections 89513 through
Agsle ror details on specific expenditures.
Saving the funds for the next election canpaign is- reasonably
refateJ io-a political 
purpose and therefore a permissible. use of
the funds, if the candidate was successful'
that the candidate must transfer the funds to
2 new Campaign。  (SeCtion 8520■
.)
when he openS the account fOr th(
Please note, however,
the new bank account
(Section 831-14; Regulation 18329(c) (3) ')


our FiIe No
Page 3
I-9o-766
A successful candidate may make a donation or loan to
nonprofit organizations under Section 89515:
Carnpaign funds may be used to make
donations or loans to bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious, or sinilar tax-
exempt, nonprofj-t organizations, where no
substantial part of the proceeds will have a
material financial effect on the candidate,
elected officer, nember of his or her
imrnediate family, or the campaign treasurer,
and $/here the donation or loan bears a
reasonable relation to a political
legislative, or governmental purpose.
You have not provided enough information
organization to a1low us to determine whether
standard enunciated in Section 89515.
An unsuccessful candidate is limited in
surplus funds. Section 89519 provides:
about the nonprofit
it meets the
how he or she may use
Upon leaving any elected office, or at
the end of the postelection reporting period
fotlowing the defeat of a candidate for
el,ective office, whichever occurs last,
carnpaign funds raised after January l, 1989,
under the control of the former candidate or
elected officer shal} be considered surpl'us
canpaign funds and shal1 be disclosed pursuant
to -hapter 4 (commencing with section 84100)
and shil1 be used only for the following
purposes 3
(a) The paynent of outstanding campaign
debts or elected officer's 
expenses.
(b) The Pro rata rePayment of
contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide
charitaLle, educational', civic, religrious or
siurilar tax-exempt, nonprofit organization,
where no substanlial pait of the Proceeds will
have a material financial effect on the former
candidate or elected offi'cer, any nenber of
his or her inrnediate fanily, or his or her
campaign treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a political 
party

to a candidate for elective office'

(e) contributions to support or oppose
any candidate for federal office, any
' 
candidate for elective office in a state other
than california, or any ballot Deasure-
Thus under Section 8951-9, the candidate nay make a donation
to a nonprofit organization under the same conditions as a
successful candidate, except that the unsuccessful candidate need
not demonstrate that the donation is reasonabl'y reLated to a
legistati.ve, governmental, or political purpose. Again,.we do not
have enough inforrnation to deterrnine whether the nonprofit
organization meets the standard set forth above.
An unsuccessful candidate may not save the surplus funds for
another election because that is not one of the permissible uses
Iisted in Section 89519.
A ballot measure committee may terminate its status as a
committee, and thus avoid further filing obligations only by
fiting a statement of Termination declaring, under penalty of
perjury, that the candidate or cornmittee:
(1) Has ceased to receive contributions and make
expenditures and does not anticipate receiving contributions
or making expenditures in the future;
(2) Has eliminated or has declared that it has no
intention or ability to discharge all of its debts, l-oans
received and other obligations.
(3) Has no surPlus funds; and
(4) Has filed all required campaign statements
disclosing all reportable transactions '
Regulation 18404 , coPY enclosed'
Thus if the comittee wishes to terninate its status as a
"ororniite", it roust fiist 
clispose of its leftover funds' Donating
a;;-.;-;'"onpiorit 
organizalion is pernitted' 
rt must file 
a
semi-annual statement (either Form 4rO or Form 450) to rePort the
ai=p".itio" of leftover funds and any other rePortable
tra^nsactions. Thereafter it may file a ForI[ 415, Statement of
reinination, if the iour criteria listed above in Regrulation 18404
are met.


our File No l-9o-7 66
' 
Page 5
I trust this letter adequately addresses your questions. If
you have any further questions regarding thj's matter, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincere IY ,
scott Hallabrin
